question_text: During evaporation, water changes from liquid to gas. Calculate the approximate number of particles that escape from a puddle if 2 grams of water evaporates. (Hint: 1 mole of water has a mass of 18 grams and contains 6 x 10^23 particles)
correct_answer: 6.67 x 10^22 particles or approximately 6.7 x 10^22 particles
explanation: If 18 grams of water contains 6 x 10^23 particles, then 2 grams contains (2/18) x (6 x 10^23) = 6.67 x 10^22 particles. These particles escape as gas during evaporation.
